question_text: Which type of electromagnetic radiation is used in medical imaging to see inside the human body without surgery, and why is it suitable for this purpose?
correct_answer: X-rays are used for medical imaging (e.g., X-ray photographs). They are suitable because they have high energy and can penetrate soft tissue but are absorbed differently by bones and dense materials, creating an image.
explanation: X-rays have short wavelengths and high energy, allowing them to pass through soft tissue but be absorbed more by dense materials like bone. This creates a useful contrast for medical diagnosis. (Note: modern hospitals also use gamma rays in PET scans and CT scanners.)
